review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-110-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: SERVICE_TITLES template is index-cycled per micro-phase, producing repeated placeholder titles across phases
findings.evidence: buildRowsFor() sets title = SERVICE_TITLES[index - 1]; RP02.P02.M04 starts at start_index 7 ("Primary happy path") while RP02.P02.M05 and RP02.P02.M06 start at index 1 ("Service entrypoint contract"), so titles like "Service entrypoint contract" and "State transition enforcement" recur in multiple micro-phases (distinct catalog_id and source_unit_ids).
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout; titles are synthetic catalog stand-ins, not literal unit descriptions. Optionally note this in README so future readers do not treat per-row titles as authoritative unit names.

findings.title: Matter-trace precheck only blocks when both matter ids are present
findings.evidence: executePermissionKernelCp110Workflow gates with `if (normalized.matter_id && normalized.resource.matter_id && normalized.matter_id !== normalized.resource.matter_id)`; a null on either side bypasses the drift check, though normalize defaults can produce null resource.matter_id.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for synthetic, evaluation-only scope (cannot compare a missing matter). Tighten in RP03+ if real matter binding is enforced; the matter_trace_blocks_before_permission_evaluation boundary is otherwise satisfied for the both-present case and covered by tests.

parsed_review.rationale: All in-scope requirements are met. evaluatePermission() is invoked only after synthetic, tenant-boundary, and matter-trace guards pass; non-synthetic, cross-tenant, and matter-drift requests return status blocked_before_permission_evaluation with evaluator_invoked=false. decision_routes map allow/deny/review_required/approval_required to completed_metadata_only/blocked_claim_output/review_required_routing/approval_required_routing in both the contract JSON and source, and match the executed-workflow tests. Idempotency, lock, persistence, rollback, and retry are emitted as metadata-only receipts (persisted/acquired/executed=false, mode metadata_only|reference_only); audit_hint is preview_only_until_rp03 with emitted_to_audit_ledger=false. The no_write_attestation (source and contract) sets all 15 flags false, including writes_audit_event, writes_product_state, creates_database_rows, executes_ai_retrieval, executes_export_download, executes_external_share, executes_claude_review, and implements_ldip; no actual write, share, export, AI, or LDIP code is present. HRX stays embedded (hrx_embedded_boundary='embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os'). Coverage is 40 units RP02.P02.M04.S07 to RP02.P02.M06.S02 (16/22/2 across M04/M05/M06) with deliverable counts (contract 2, implementation 16, security_audit 2, ui 6, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 4, test 8) matching validators, and handoff is fixed to CP00-111 / RP02.P02.M06.S03. The two P3 items are cosmetic/non-blocking.
notes: No P0/P1/P2 findings were reported by the valid CP00-110 pack-level Claude review.
P3 finding about repeated service template titles was documented in packages/authz/README.md.
P3 finding about missing matter trace handling was fixed by fail-closed normalization plus tests and validator assertions.
No second valid Claude review was run.
